hero.headline.value: AI agents ship code. I write the contracts that keep them in check.

hero.intro.value: I'm Bobby. I build the controls that keep AI agents inside the job you gave them.

hero.summary.value: Senior engineer at Aura, where I built the controls for shipping AI-written code after unreviewed agent commits reached main. I open-sourced the approach as prx. Agent changes land as merged PRs through one signed flow. Each git write is checked against the capability that holds it.

seeking.focus.value: Senior / founding engineering on agent infrastructure: agent safety and code provenance

seeking.detail.value: Senior engineering on teams building agentic products. I bring direct experience shipping agent-authored systems and the controls that make them trustworthy. Brooklyn · NYC · remote.

exp.aura.what.value: On Commerce, the team behind Aura's website, commerce-routing, and business-operations systems, running on a Ruby backend across fulfillment, accounting, and third-party integrations. I ship the automation backbone on AWS (ECS, RDS, S3, CloudFront) and built the contract-and-validation layer behind inventory-purchasing.

exp.aura.b0.value: Wrote the CLI that replaced Aura's 9-tab spreadsheet supply plan with a single source of truth. It compiles to 61 downstream schema contracts, checked by 173 test scenarios.

exp.aura.b1.value: Automated the inbound supply chain — ocean freight into the 3PL, Amazon LTL, Tactical→Amazon splits — replacing manual coordination with schema-structured pipelines.

exp.aura.b2.value: Investigated unreviewed agent-authored commits reaching main. Reconstructed each one's path across five log domains and wrote the post-mortem. Designed the controls that became Aura's policy for shipping AI-written code.

exp.l2l.what.value: Owned the quality gates and developer experience for a 20-engineer team on a Python/Django backend, deployed single-tenant per customer (EC2/ECS, RDS, S3, Lambda). Drove static-analysis CI, a design system, and frontend performance.

exp.l2l.b0.value: Shipped static-analysis gating into the PR pipeline. It blocks whole defect classes at merge instead of catching them after. Critical bugs fell 20% across the team.

exp.pioneer-works.what.value: Built the event-planning platform's backend for a major Brooklyn arts center — Python and JavaScript on AWS Lambda and Postgres, integrating Square POS over webhooks.

exp.pioneer-works.b0.value: Labeled event-state transitions by the department that emitted each POS event, not a human-set flag, across the venue's large concerts.

exp.kaleida.what.value: Built the just-in-time production automation for an AR and print-making studio on Postgres, with heavy third-party API integration.

exp.kaleida.b0.value: Eliminated edition-numbering errors. A database issued serial edition IDs on request, making duplicates impossible by construction.

exp.scope-of-work.what.value: Built the job-postings CMS behind the manufacturing publication's newsletter, and its customer segmentation, on Postgres — integrating outside tools through their APIs.

exp.scope-of-work.b0.value: Designed the schema that moved spreadsheet data into a relational database, so a malformed record couldn't be written.

exp.recurse-center.what.value: Type theory in Haskell, using the type system to make invalid states unrepresentable. It's the root of the capability work: push correctness to the boundary, and violations can't compile.

exp.recurse-center.b0.value: Completed a selective 12-week programming retreat. Researched how a tight type constraint narrows the space of valid implementations.

proj.prx.desc.value: An agent pipeline that drives each work unit to a merged PR. Git-writes are verified against the capability owner that holds them. Evolved from hooksmith, a Rust predecessor that still anchors Bounded Systems' lower-level, machine-facing contracts. Moved to TypeScript once the key contracts proved to be between systems, not machine and kernel.

proj.ocap-provenance.desc.value: Provenance extended with object-capability semantics, built in TypeScript. Each attestation records who produced an artifact and which capabilities they held — and which were denied.

work.prx.value: The agent-run work-unit CLI: capability-scoped agents whose privileged effects are verified against their signed owner, driving a work unit through one signed pipeline to a merged PR.

work.guest-room.value: Guest-agnostic room+door capability runtime — an agent reaches the outside world only through the door references it's handed.

work.claude-box.value: A capability-secured box for agent sessions — its authority is the door references it holds (keeper/scout/concierge/net), parent-agnostic.

work.door-kit.value: In-box door-client SDK for claude-box's capability doors (keeper/scout/concierge/spawn), over the guest-room protocol.

work.ocap-provenance.value: Capability-use provenance — a schema + SLSA mapping binding each privileged effect to a signed owner and an auditable chain.

work.string-audit.value: Cost-aware, grounded content auditor — typed string symbols, type-scoped audits, CAS-memoized LLM calls.

copy.colophon.more.value: Hermetic Nix build · keyless-signed in-toto/SLSA provenance (Sigstore · Rekor · GHCR) —

copy.colophon.lede.value: The upstream tools that produce and validate this site — each a real build input or gate, not a credit list. See /provenance for the full signed chain.

copy.blog.lede.value: On capability security for agentic systems — the thesis, graded against the running code.

copy.prov.lede.value: This site is built deterministically from versioned sources and validated at every boundary — the same discipline the work itself argues for. Here's what produced and checked this artifact.

copy.prov.chain.lede.value: The build reads as an in-toto / SLSA-style provenance: declared materials, a checked build process, and a signed subject. Each link is verified; the last is the artifact itself.

copy.conf.lede.value: A live, computed conformance report — not a badge. The build folds its own evidence (the gates that ran, the artifacts it signs) through lone's web-build conformance model. The strong claim is withheld unless every required criterion is met; anything not yet assessed says so, plainly.

copy.head.provenance.desc.value: How robertdelanghe.dev is built and validated — contracts, gates, and claim-to-evidence.

copy.head.conformance.desc.value: A computed, honest conformance report for robertdelanghe.dev — lone's web-build model over this build's evidence; unsupplied criteria are reported as not-assessed, never overclaimed.

copy.head.colophon.desc.value: The upstream tools that build and validate robertdelanghe.dev — each a real build input or gate.

copy.head.blog.desc.tail.value: on capability security for agentic systems.
